title: [1.4.2-stable] Vendor in c/storage fix on double-locking mounts list

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *umohnani8* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/v1.4.2-stable/OWNERS)~~ [umohnani8] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

LGTM.

@nalind @rhatdan Can we get a tagged release of c/storage with this patch included for the new Podman 1.6 release on Friday?
@mheon @baude tests are green, can we get this. Will we need to tag a new v1.4.2-stable or can @lsm5 create and tag a new build from this branch?

> @mheon @baude tests are green, can we get this. Will we need to tag a new v1.4.2-stable or can @lsm5 create and tag a new build from this branch?

tag would help to keep things cleaner of course, but not totally necessary.
I'd prefer not to make an additional tag in the 1.4.2-stable branch if this is not hitting 8.1
@mheon what do you suggest then? should we create a specific ocp tag like  v1.4.2-stable-ocp? or should we just create a build from the v1.4.2-stable branch?

> @mheon what do you suggest then? should we create a specific ocp tag like v1.4.2-stable-ocp? or should we just create a build from the v1.4.2-stable branch?

i can just build from here. Btw, I have a PR coming in that I'd like to get merged on this branch as well.
@lsm5 I think they want to pin to 1.4.2-stable to keep rough parity with 8.1, so I think this patch on top of the head of the 1.4.2 branch is what you want
Oh, this is targetted against 1.4.2-stable already. Alright, we can merge and build from there.
